missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Cecil B. deAldrin is standing by for instructions.

missing_communications.speaker: CDR
missing_communications.text: Roger. That's verified; the probe is extended.

missing_communications.speaker: CDR
missing_communications.text: That EMS DELTA-V counter is minus 4.0.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Houston, Apollo 11.LMP is back on the line.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger, 11. You're about one-by. Go ahead.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger. We copy. Standby a minute, please.

missing_communications.speaker: CMP
missing_communications.text: Okay. Fine. Let ts try that.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger. I just wanted to remind you that we haven't noticed on the TM the VERB 66 after the burn. And for your information, we played the recorded TV back last night, I believe, after you all turned in for your rest period, and the pictures came out quite well. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Goldstone, this is Houston CAP C0MM. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Negative. Standby. We wanted to know - -

missing_communications.speaker: CT
missing_communications.text: Houston CAP COMM, Goldstone M&0 NET 1.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger. You're five-by. Is Neil on?

missing_communications.speaker: CMP
missing_communications.text: Correction. Stand by; that's not so.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger, we copy. Radiation 11005, 10006, 09007; no medication; 37.1, 37.1, 37.1, 82, 84, 85, 87. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CMP
missing_communications.text: Start after the rains in Houston.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: That sounds fine to us. Ovcr.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Now you know how we feel.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Okay. Bravo, 0MNI; TRACK, MANUAL; and BEAM, WIDE.

missing_communications.speaker: CMP
missing_communications.text: Because it's a special occasion today, Houston. This is the third anniversary of Gemini 10.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger. Reading you five by. How me? Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CMP
missing_communications.text: Okay. Will do. I don't see any constraint here, Charlie. I was just checking to make sure, because last time, I went from 8 to 9 to 10 to 11 a little bit more swiftly than I'd been doing in the past.

missing_communications.speaker: CMP
missing_communications.text: Houston, Apollo 11. How's the PTC looking?

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Roger. On sleep; CDR, CMP, 7.5; LMP, 6.5. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger. We missed an 0MNI switch there. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: 11, this is Houston. Readhack correct. Out.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger. You'll be crossing 45 west at 77 04 10 or about 40 seconds from now. Over. Thirty seconds from now.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger. I have a comment here that says that's what the LM was built for.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger, Mike. It's landmark Alfa 1: T1, 82 37 35; T2, 82 42 50. We're 7 miles north. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CMP
missing_communications.text: Copy. P22: T1, time 82 37 35; T2, 82 42 50. And the target is 7 miles north. Thank you.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5; 5, 4, 3, 2, 1. We're reading you five by. You can go to high bit rate now. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger. Reading you five by also, Buzz, and we got the high bit rate. It's looking beautiful through Goldstone. Giving you a count: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5; 5, 4, 3, 2, 1. Please give us a count. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger. You're beautiful in this mode, Buzz. We're reading you five by. Coming with a short count and we'd like one back from you. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5; 5, 4, 3, 2, 1. Houston out.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Houston, Eagle. About four by four. Go ahead.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger, Mike. Coming at you with the 130, P1 is 98 37 35, P2 98 42 44, 4 miles north. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CMP
missing_communications.text: Do you want the T EPHEM first?

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Houston, Eagle, LMP. How do you read? Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger. Five by, Buzz. How me? Over.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Roger. Standing by for your K factor.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Eagle, Houston. We recommend the AGS initial - Well, we see it coming up now on the AGS initialization. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Apollo 11, Houston. One minute to LOS.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Go ahead, Houston. Eagle is ready to copy.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: That's affirmative. Go ahead with the PDI.

missing_communications.speaker: CMP
missing_communications.text: Yes. I'm oscillating between 0.69 and seven tenths.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: That's affirmative, Eagle. Reading you five by.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Eagle, Houston. Stand by for my Mark.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Houston, Eagle. How do you read?

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: It's looking good to us. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Eagle, you're looking great. Coming up 9 minutes.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: 40 feet, down 2 1/2. Kicking up some dust.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: - - T1. Over. Eagle, you are STAY for T1.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger. And we see you venting the 0X.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Like an AGS to PNGS align. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CDR
missing_communications.text: Roger. We got it; thank you.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Okay. Here comes the E memory dump.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Say again, Mike. You're about two by.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Columbia, Houston. First of all, we'd like a waste water dump to 10 percent on the backside. Secondly, it does not look like we are going to need any plane change at this time, so we will not be uplinking a new REFSMMAT. Third item, I would like all of your CRYO heaters to AUTO, and we are ready for a battery charge, battery BRAVO; it will last about 7 hours. If you should go to sleep, we will be terminating that BATT charge, but at the moment, we can go ahead and start the BATT charge on BATT Bravo. And a final item, for your SM RCS configuration for your rest period, register 1 for the DAP is 11111; DAP register 2, 01100. And your AUTO RCS select

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Tranquility Base, Tranquility Base, this is Houston. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: LMP is reading 09017 and three quarters.

missing_communications.speaker: CMP
missing_communications.text: Affirmative. When's hatch open time in GET estinated?

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger, Mike. Papa 0.2 and 6.3 on your LAM 2 chart. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CMP
missing_communications.text: Thank you. Papa 0.2 and 6.3. I'll try them.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Okay. I think that's going to be better.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: I got another warning tone. Go.

missing_communications.speaker: CDR
missing_communications.text: Houston, Tranquility. How do you read ? Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: We're reading you loud and clear, Buzz. Out.

missing_communications.speaker: CDR
missing_communications.text: *** both suit isolation valves to SUIT DISCONNECT.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Oh, wait a minute. Should be - , Stand by.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: *** Got your mikes where you want them?

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Now, if you'll pull the RCU down.

missing_communications.speaker: CDR
missing_communications.text: Open my RCU there, would you, Buzz?

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: No. I'd better get up first.

missing_communications.speaker: CDR
missing_communications.text: Okay. Houston, I'm on the porch.

missing_communications.speaker: CDR
missing_communications.text: Houston, the MESA came down all right.

missing_communications.speaker: CDR
missing_communications.text: Houston, this is Neil. Radio check.

missing_communications.speaker: CDR
missing_communications.text: For those who haven't read the plaque, we'll read the plaque that's on the front landing gear of this LM. First there's two hemispheres, one showing each of the two hemispheres of the Earth. Underneath it says "Here Man from the planet Earth first set foot upon the Moon, July 1969 A.D. We came in peace for all mankind." It has the crew members' signatures and the signature of the President of the United States.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Oh, it's beautiful, Mike. It really is.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Go ahead, Mr. President. This is Houston. Out.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Taking some close up pictures of that rock.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Columbia. This is Houston. Grid coordinates: Kilo 0.9, 6.3, on LAM 2. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CDR
missing_communications.text: Houston, Tranquility Base. The CDR's PRD reads 11014.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger. When you get back into your surface checklist and come over to the COMM reconfiguration on page Surface 45, we'd like you to enable the ranging feature on your S band. That is, when you come down to S band configuration, instead of CAUTION and WARNING ELECTRONICS, ENABLE TV, we'd like you to go into the RANGE position and leave it there for as long as you conveniently can until you get ready to commence your rest period; and we'll try to get a little more ranging data on you. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Columbia, Colunbia, this is Houston. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CDR
missing_communications.text: Roger. Go ahead with the K factor.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Tranquility Base. Roger. We verify; those are correct.

missing_communications.speaker: CMP
missing_communications.text: Houston, Columbia on 0MNI D Delta. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Roger. We copy and we agree.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: 9, 8, 7, 6, 5, abort stage, engine arm ascent, proceed.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: S band looks like it's holding good, Houston.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: - - this is H dot MAX now.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: *** want to get another *** data update ***

missing_communications.speaker: CMP
missing_communications.text: That was a funny one. You know, I didn't feel it shock, and I thought things were pretty steady. I went to RETRACT there, and that's when all hell broke loose. Were you guys - did it appear to you to be - that you were jerking around quite a bit during the retrack cycle?

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: Hey, Columbia. Houston. Mighty fine; loud and clear.

missing_communications.speaker: CMP
missing_communications.text: Okay. Understand. I'm going to go to roll 1.1, pitch zero - or 52.6, and yaw 13.8, and you want a waste water dump 10 percent starting at 131 05.

missing_communications.speaker: CMP
missing_communications.text: BATT Charlie 370, PYRO BATT A 370, BATT B 370; RCS, 55, 65, 64, and 62. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: CC
missing_communications.text: 11, Houston. Reading you about one by. Over.

missing_communications.speaker: LMP
missing_communications.text: Roger. I copy, and our onboard readouts: Alfa, *** 2 percent, Bravo 54 percent, Cocoa's 64 percent, Delta 61 percent. Over.
